summary: Fiery Garcia, strong second set, standout Eyes > Wharf
blurb: Jerry’s guitar is on fire and the second set flows from Estimated Prophet into a soaring Eyes of the World. Keith’s electric piano adds texture, while the dual‑drummer mix draws mixed reactions. Overall a solid late‑’77 performance worth hearing.
review: The September 28, 1977 Paramount show captures the Dead in top form. Jerry Garcia delivers his trademark fiery lead—his Wharf Rat solo is described as “hauntingly beautiful,” and his improvisations on Eyes of the World are a high point that keeps the band locked in. Keith Godchaux’s electric piano shines through the jams, earning praise as “pretty dope.” The second set is the real engine room: Estimated Prophet segues into an expansive Eyes of the World, rolls into a crisp drums‑Not Fade Away transition and builds to a powerful Wharf Rat before closing with Around & Around. While Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming earns kudos for tightness, Mickey Hart’s playing can feel “clogged” or overly loud in some mixes, giving the dual‑drummer setup a mixed reputation. The Betty Board soundboard (gd1977-09-28.sbd.cantor) offers crystal‑clear audio, but the Connor SBD capture is still the recommended source for its overall balance despite missing the first five songs. Fans looking for a classic late‑’70s set with strong jams and memorable solos will find this night a rewarding listen.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two of three recordings are clearly positive, highlighting standout performances (Eyes of the World, Wharf Rat, Estimated Prophet) and strong band chemistry; one recording is mixed but its criticisms focus on drum balance rather than musical execution. The overall consensus is a strong, engaging show, justifying a high positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Good soundboard capture with clear mix and balanced instruments; despite missing the first five songs, it offers the most reliable audio quality among available sources.

key_highlights: Jerry’s incendiary lead work—especially his Wharf Rat solo and Eyes of the World jam.
A seamless second‑set flow from Estimated Prophet through Eyes of the World into Not Fade Away and Wharf Rat.
Keith Godchaux’s electric piano adds a “pretty dope” texture that lifts the jams.
Dual drummers generate mixed reactions: Bill praised for tightness, Mickey sometimes clogs the groove.

band_performance.Jerry: Fiery, rapid guitar work throughout; standout solo on Wharf Rat and soaring leads on Eyes of the World.
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation with a plunking feel; adds a light‑hearted spoken interjection near the encore.
band_performance.Bob: Vocals and rhythm guitar praised as a great contribution to the night’s energy.
band_performance.Brent: Keith Godchaux’s electric piano is highlighted as ‘pretty dope,’ especially during jam sections.
band_performance.Mickey: Often criticized for being too loud and occasionally clogging the groove, making some passages feel plodding.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann receives praise for tight playing in the second recording, though his drums can sound loud in the first SBD.
